payload.description:
As a score optimizer
I want to run iterative improvement cycles with human approval
So that I can progressively improve alignment while maintaining control

## Acceptance Criteria

- Analyzer agent reviews RCA and proposes changes
- HITL approval gate blocks before applying changes
- New score version created with change hypothesis
- Evaluation runs with fixed baseline comparison
- Metric deltas calculated correctly
- Iteration continues until stop criteria met

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: Iterative Optimization with Human-in-the-Loop
  As a score optimizer
  I want to iteratively improve a score with human oversight
  So that I can achieve better alignment safely

Scenario: Single optimization iteration with approval
  Given a baseline evaluation exists for score "empathy"
  And the baseline AC1 is 0.75
  When the analyzer agent reviews the RCA output
  Then a hypothesis and proposed YAML changes are generated
  And a HITL approval gate prompts for review with:
    | Field      | Present |
    | Hypothesis | Yes     |
    | Changes    | Yes     |
    | Rationale  | Yes     |
  When I approve the proposed changes
  Then a new score version is created with the hypothesis in version_note
  And a feedback evaluation runs with baseline comparison
  And metric deltas are calculated:
    | Metric    | Calculated |
    | AC1 delta | Yes        |
    | Accuracy delta | Yes   |
  And the iteration log records all details

Scenario: User rejects proposed changes
  Given a baseline evaluation exists
  And the analyzer proposes changes
  When the HITL approval gate appears
  And I reject the proposed changes
  Then no score version is created
  And the procedure stops gracefully
  And the status is set to "user_stopped"

Scenario: Multiple iterations until convergence
  Given a baseline evaluation with AC1=0.75
  And max_iterations is set to 5
  And improvement_threshold is 0.02
  When iteration 1 improves AC1 to 0.78 (delta=+0.03)
  And iteration 2 improves AC1 to 0.80 (delta=+0.02)
  And iteration 3 improves AC1 to 0.805 (delta=+0.005)
  Then a HITL gate asks whether to continue (below threshold)
  When I choose to stop
  Then the procedure completes with status="improvement_plateau"
  And 3 iterations are logged

Scenario: Fixed baseline tracking
  Given a baseline evaluation ID "eval-baseline-123"
  When multiple iterations run
  Then all evaluations use baseline="eval-baseline-123"
  And all deltas are calculated against the original baseline metrics
  And not against the previous iteration metrics
```
payload.title: Iterative optimization loop with HITL gates
